From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

(Redirected from [Construction permit](/w/index.php?title=Construction_permit&redirect=no "Construction permit"))

Government permission required for construction or expansion

For planning permission laws in the UK, see [Planning permission in the United Kingdom](/wiki/Planning_permission_in_the_United_Kingdom "Planning permission in the United Kingdom").

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/8/8e/One57_under_construction_3_November_2012.jpeg/250px-One57_under_construction_3_November_2012.jpeg)](/wiki/File:One57_under_construction_3_November_2012.jpeg)

[One57](/wiki/One57 "One57"), a skyscraper in [New York City](/wiki/New_York_City "New York City"), under construction. Such a development would have gone through stringent checks against the local building code before planning permission was granted.

**Planning permission** or **building permit** refers to the approval needed for construction or expansion (including significant [renovation](/wiki/Renovation "Renovation")), and sometimes for demolition, in some jurisdictions.[[1]](#cite_note-1)[[2]](#cite_note-2)

House building permits, for example, are subject to [building codes](/wiki/Building_code "Building code"). There is also a "plan check" (PLCK) to check compliance with plans for the area, if any.[[3]](#cite_note-plck1-3) For example, one cannot obtain permission to build a nightclub in an area where it is inappropriate such as a high-density suburb.[[4]](#cite_note-4)[[5]](#cite_note-5) The criteria for planning permission are a part of [urban planning](/wiki/Urban_planning "Urban planning") and [construction law](/wiki/Construction_law "Construction law"), and are usually managed by [town planners](/wiki/Town_planner "Town planner") employed by [local governments](/wiki/Local_government "Local government").[[6]](#cite_note-6)[[7]](#cite_note-7)

Failure to obtain a permit can result in [fines](/wiki/Fine_(penalty) "Fine (penalty)"), [penalties](/wiki/Sanctions_(law) "Sanctions (law)"), and [demolition](/wiki/Demolition "Demolition") of unauthorized construction if it cannot be made to meet code. Generally, the new construction must be [inspected](/wiki/Building_inspection "Building inspection") during construction and after completion to ensure compliance with national, regional, and local [building codes](/wiki/Building_code "Building code"). Since building permits usually precede outlays for construction, employment, financing and furnishings, they are often used as a [leading indicator](/wiki/Economic_indicator "Economic indicator") for developments in other areas of the economy.

The number of building permits issued per year varies by country.[[8]](#cite_note-8) By-right approval processes can be faster than discretionary approval processes.[[9]](#cite_note-9)

## In specific industries

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Planning_permission&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: In specific industries")]

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [[icon]](/wiki/File:Wiki_letter_w_cropped.svg) | This section **needs expansion**. You can help by [adding missing information](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Planning_permission&action=edit&section=). *(October 2021)* |

### Broadcasting

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Planning_permission&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Broadcasting")]

As part of [broadcast law](/wiki/Broadcast_law "Broadcast law"), the term is also used in [broadcasting](/wiki/Broadcasting "Broadcasting"), where individual [radio](/wiki/Radio_station "Radio station") and [television stations](/wiki/Television_station "Television station") typically must apply for and receive permission to construct [radio towers](/wiki/Radio_masts_and_towers "Radio masts and towers") and [radio antennas](/wiki/Radio_antenna "Radio antenna"). This type of permit is issued by a national broadcasting authority, but does *not* imply [zoning](/wiki/Zoning "Zoning") any other permission that must be given by [local government](/wiki/Local_government "Local government"). The permit itself also does not necessarily imply permission to operate the station once constructed. In the U.S., a construction permit is valid for three years. Afterwards, the station must receive a full license to operate, which is good for seven years.[[10]](#cite_note-10) This is provided by a separate [broadcast license](/wiki/Broadcast_license "Broadcast license"), also called a "license to cover" by the [Federal Communications Commission](/wiki/Federal_Communications_Commission "Federal Communications Commission") (FCC) in the United States. Further permission or registration for [towers](/wiki/Tower "Tower") may be needed from [aviation](/wiki/Aviation "Aviation") authorities.

In the U.S., construction permits for new commercial stations are now assigned by auction, rather than the former process of determining who would serve the [community of license](/wiki/Community_of_license "Community of license") best. If the given [frequency allocation](/wiki/Frequency_allocation "Frequency allocation") is sought by at least one [non-commercial educational](/wiki/Non-commercial_educational "Non-commercial educational") (NCE) applicant, or is on an NCE-reserved TV channel or in the [FM](/wiki/FM_broadcasting "FM broadcasting") reserved band, the comparative process still takes place, though the FCC refuses to consider which [radio format](/wiki/Radio_format "Radio format") the applicants propose.

In Canada, the [Canadian Radio-television and Telecommunications Commission](/wiki/Canadian_Radio-television_and_Telecommunications_Commission "Canadian Radio-television and Telecommunications Commission") maintains a comparative process in issuing permits, ensuring that a variety of programming is available in each area, and that as many groups as possible have access to [free speech](/wiki/Free_speech "Free speech") over [radio waves](/wiki/Radio_waves "Radio waves").

## See also

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Planning_permission&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: See also")]

* [Corruption in local government](/wiki/Corruption_in_local_government "Corruption in local government")
